remediation.title: Kerberoastable Account
remediation.description: The account has a Service Principal Name set and its TGS ticket can be requested by any domain user for offline cracking.
steps: Verify whether the service account actually requires an SPN.
If not, remove it: Set-ADUser -Clear ServicePrincipalName.
If required, set a random password of at least 25 characters.
Consider migrating to a Managed Service Account (MSA) or gMSA.

remediation.title: AS-REP Roastable Account
remediation.description: Kerberos pre-authentication is disabled, allowing an attacker to request an AS-REP and crack it offline.
steps: In ADUC, uncheck 'Do not require Kerberos preauthentication'.
Investigate why this setting was disabled; restrict access if a legacy app requires it.

remediation.title: Kerberoastable Account
remediation.description: The account has a Service Principal Name set and its TGS ticket can be requested by any domain user for offline cracking.
steps: Verify whether the service account actually requires an SPN.
If not, remove it: Set-ADUser -Clear ServicePrincipalName.
If required, set a random password of at least 25 characters.
Consider migrating to a Managed Service Account (MSA) or gMSA.

remediation.title: Dangerous Delegation Configuration
remediation.description: Unconstrained or constrained Kerberos delegation is configured, allowing credential forwarding attacks.
steps: Identify unconstrained delegation: Get-ADComputer/Get-ADUser -Filter {TrustedForDelegation -eq $true}
Replace unconstrained delegation with constrained delegation where possible.
Add sensitive accounts to the Protected Users group to block delegation entirely.

remediation.title: SMB Signing Not Required
remediation.description: SMB signing is not enforced, exposing the network to relay and man-in-the-middle attacks.
steps: GPO: Computer Configuration > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options > 'Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (always)' = Enabled.
Apply to both domain controllers and member servers.
Audit existing SMB connections before enforcing to avoid disruption.

remediation.title: AdminSDHolder Anomaly
remediation.description: Account has adminCount=1 but is not a member of any privileged group, indicating a residual or misconfigured access right.
steps: Identify accounts with adminCount=1 that are not members of any privileged group.
Manually reset adminCount to 0 on those accounts.
Verify the DACL is corrected after the SDProp task runs.

remediation.title: LDAP Signing Not Required
remediation.description: LDAP signing is not enforced on the domain controller, enabling LDAP relay attacks.
steps: Verify KB4520412 and later updates are applied to all DCs.
GPO: 'Domain controller: LDAP server channel binding token requirements' = Always.
Monitor Event ID 3039/3040 for channel binding failures.

remediation.title: LAPS Not Deployed
remediation.description: Local Administrator Password Solution is not installed; local admin passwords may be shared or stale.
steps: Enable Microsoft LAPS or Windows LAPS (Windows Server 2019+) via GPO.
Install ms-Mcs-AdmPwd / msLAPS-Password schema extensions.
Link the GPO to the affected OUs and run gpupdate /force.

remediation.title: LAPS Not Deployed

remediation.title: Weak Password Policy
remediation.description: The domain password policy has weak settings that allow short, simple, or reused passwords.
steps: Set minimum password length to at least 12 characters.
Enable password complexity requirements.
Set password history length to at least 10.

remediation.title: Stale Account Detected
remediation.description: The account has not logged in for over 90 days and represents an unnecessary attack surface.
steps: List accounts: Search-ADAccount -AccountInactive -TimeSpan 90.00:00:00
Disable accounts with business unit approval: Disable-ADAccount.
After a retention period, permanently remove with Remove-ADUser.
